verses.text: 'Seek ye the LORD, all ye meek of the earth... seek righteousness, seek meekness: it may be ye shall be hid' in the day of anger. A door of hope. The humble who seek God may find shelter from the storm.

'Seek ye the LORD, all ye meek of the earth... seek righteousness, seek meekness: it may be ye shall be hid' in the day of anger. A door of hope. The humble who seek God may find shelter from the storm.

'Seek ye the LORD, all ye meek of the earth... seek righteousness, seek meekness: it may be ye shall be hid' in the day of anger. A door of hope. The humble who seek God may find shelter from the storm.
Gaza, Ashkelon, and the Philistine coast shall be desolate; the remnant of Judah shall pasture there. Enemies cleared. God removes the old oppressors to bless His people.
Gaza, Ashkelon, and the Philistine coast shall be desolate; the remnant of Judah shall pasture there. Enemies cleared. God removes the old oppressors to bless His people.
Gaza, Ashkelon, and the Philistine coast shall be desolate; the remnant of Judah shall pasture there. Enemies cleared. God removes the old oppressors to bless His people.
Gaza, Ashkelon, and the Philistine coast shall be desolate; the remnant of Judah shall pasture there. Enemies cleared. God removes the old oppressors to bless His people.
He has heard the reproach of Moab and Ammon; they shall be 'as Sodom,' a possession for His remnant. Pride judged. Those who mocked God's people inherit their shame.
He has heard the reproach of Moab and Ammon; they shall be 'as Sodom,' a possession for His remnant. Pride judged. Those who mocked God's people inherit their shame.
This is for their pride; God will 'famish all the gods of the earth,' and men worship Him from every isle. Idols starved. The fall of false gods turns the nations to the LORD.
This is for their pride; God will 'famish all the gods of the earth,' and men worship Him from every isle. Idols starved. The fall of false gods turns the nations to the LORD.

Ethiopia shall be slain, and Assyria destroyed; Nineveh, who boasted 'I am, and there is none beside me,' shall be desolate. Boasting silenced. The proud city that claimed God's own title is laid waste.
